low_side_arcs:
CONVENTIONS (all verified in sympy, scripts in /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-mruckman1-Desktop-dev-quantum-gravity/31217463-cf79-4bf7-a0e6-c2b5dbd908b3/scratchpad/check1_lowside.py etc.): CMRS 2102.08951 works at fixed u with s the dispersed variable and t=-s-u; our machinery fixes t and disperses s — identical structure under the relabel t<->u. CMRS eq (23) g-side rule is VERBATIM our residue rule: C_{k,u}|low = Res_{s'=0}[ (2s'+u)/(s'(s'+u)) * M_low(s',u) / (s'(s'+u))^{k/2} ], heavy side = <(2m^2+u)/(m^2+u) * P_J(1+2u/m^2)/[m^2(m^2+u)]^{k/2}> with the heavy average eq (18): <.> = (1/pi) sum_{J even} n_J^(D) int_{M^2}^inf dm^2/m^2 m^{4-D} rho_J(m^2). So qgse's g-side engine carries over unchanged; only M_low and the t-treatment change.

EXACT PER-TERM RESIDUE TABLE (sympy-verified, exact, D-independent; G8 := 8*pi*G; u is the fixed variable):
k=2 (eq 39): G-term 8piG[st/u+su/t+tu/s] -> total -G8/u = 8piG/(-u). Piece-by-piece (the bookkeeping that must be unit-tested): st/u -> -G8/u ; su/t -> -G8/u ; tu/s -> +G8/u ; sum = -G8/u exactly, NO analytic-in-u remainder. lambda3^2-term -lambda3^2[1/s+1/t+1/u] -> 0 exactly. lambda4 -> 0. g2*sigma2 -> +2 g2. g3*(stu) -> -g3 u. g4*sigma2^2 -> +8 g4 u^2. g5 sigma2 sigma3 -> -2 g5 u^3. g6 sigma2^3 -> +24 g6 u^4. g6' sigma3^2 -> 0. g7 sigma2^2 sigma3 -> -4 g7 u^5. TOTAL = eq (39): C_2,u = 8piG/(-u) + 2g2 - g3 u + 8g4 u^2 - 2g5 u^3 + 24 g6 u^4 - 4 g7 u^5 + ...
k=4 (eq 40): G, lambda3^2, lambda4, g2, g3 all -> 0; C_4,u = 4g4 - 2g5 u + (24g6 + g6')u^2 - 8g7 u^3 + ...
k=6 (eq 41): C_6,u = 8g6 - 4g7 u + ...; G/lambda columns again exactly 0.

LAURENT/LOG STRUCTURE AS t->0: at tree level the ONLY singular cell is k=2 at Laurent order t^{-1} with exact coefficient -8piG (i.e. B_2(t) superset -8piG/t + 2g2 - g3 t + ...). No logs at tree level for any k; log(-t) branch cuts appear only at loop level (CMRS Sec 3.1 lists this as motivation #2 for smearing), and in D=4 an IR log appears in impact-parameter space (eq 38's 8piG b/(D-4) pole -> eq 70's log(b_max)).

COLUMNS: G is a genuine new column but it hits EXACTLY ONE cell: (k=2, r=-1) with entry -8piG; it has ZERO entries in every Taylor cell (k=2, r>=0) and in all k>=4 cells (sympy-verified to the orders above). lambda3^2 and lambda4 hit NO cell of any twice-subtracted rule k>=2 — they are dispersively invisible in this system (bounding them needs k<=0 rules with improved Regge assumptions, CMRS eqs (71)-(79), a separate axiom flag). CONSEQUENCE: the (2,-1) cell lies outside the Taylor lattice, so any functional touching the C_2 family MUST be evaluated at finite u (fixed-t / smeared, non-Taylor); the k>=4 Taylor lattice is G-free and survives verbatim — our existing null machinery (n4, n5, ...) is exactly the X_k sector (verified: X_{2,u->0}[m^2,J] = [2/(D(D-2))] * Jc^2(2Jc^2-5D+4)/m^8, Jc^2 := J(J+D-3), which is eq (27); at D=4 it equals n4/(4 m^8) with n4 = 2X(X-8), X=J(J+1)). In the smeared formulation the low-side 'columns' collapse to three exact numbers per functional f(p)=sum_n a_n p^n: alpha_G = int_0^M f/p^2 = sum a_n/(n-1) (needs n>1), alpha_2 = 2 int f = 2 sum a_n/(n+1), alpha_3 = int f p^2 = sum a_n/(n+3), giving the certified inequality alpha_G*8piG + alpha_2/2*2g_2... precisely eq (47): int_0^M dp f(p)[8piG/p^2 + 2g_2 + g_3 p^2] >= 0. The h_k smearing of X_k contributes 0 to the low side (exact nulls, G-free — verified).
graviton_pole_handling:
WHY t->0 TAYLOR FAILS (three sympy/paper-verified reasons, CMRS Sec 3.1): (1) the k=2 low side is 8piG/(-u): B_2(0) and all forward derivatives are literally infinite — the (2,-1) Laurent cell cannot be represented in a Taylor-functional basis, and our g_2=<1/m^4>, g_3 rows (which live in C_2) lose their low-side anchor; (2) restricting to |u|<<M^2 to stay 'near-forward' only suppresses the pole parametrically — all higher contacts g_4 u^2, g_5 u^3, ... contaminate C_2 at |u|~M^2 (eq 39), so no sharp M-scaling bounds; (3) in impact-parameter space the forward limit is f(p)=delta(p) => fhat(b)=1, and int db fhat(b) * 8piG b/(D-4) diverges (eq 38) — positivity forces fhat>=0, so the ONLY way to suppress gravity is to localize fhat at b~1/M, which requires momentum support up to |u|~M^2.

THE CMRS RESOLUTION, two steps. Step A — 'improved' sum rule kills the contact tower EXACTLY: C_2^improved = C_2,u - sum_{n>=2}[ n u^{2n-2} C_{2n,0} + u^{2n-1} C'_{2n,0} ] (eq 42), whose EFT side is exactly 8piG/(-u) + 2g_2 - g_3 u (eq 43; sympy-verified through degree-8 couplings incl. g6'). The heavy-side per-state kernel resums in closed form (eq 44; identity verified via B_{2n}(0) = 2 P_J(1)/m^{4n}, B'_{2n}(0) = [-(2n+1)P_J(1) + 4P'_J(1)]/m^{4n+2} and the geometric series in u^2/m^4, convergent for |u|<m^2):
  C_2^imp[m^2,J](u) = (2m^2+u) P_J(1+2u/m^2) / (m^2 (m^2+u)^2)  -  (u^2/m^6) [ (4m^2+3u) P_J(1)/(m^2+u)^2  +  4u P'_J(1)/(m^4-u^2) ],
with P_J(x) = 2F1(-J, J+D-3; (D-2)/2; (1-x)/2) (eq 9), P_J(1)=1, P'_J(1)=J(J+D-3)/(D-2). Anchors (all verified symbolic-D): J=0 gives EXACTLY C_2^imp[m^2,0](-p^2) = 2/m^4 + 3p^2/m^6 (eq 56, positive, m-shape-independent); the kernel is REGULAR at u=-m^2 for every even J (double-pole and residue vanish identically, using P_J(-1)=1, P'_J(-1)=-P'_J(1) for even J) — this is why the sum rule survives on the closed range u in (-M^2, 0] and why smeared integrals have no (m^2-p^2) endpoint singularities. Validity: u in (-M^2, 0]; extension below -M^2 only under meromorphy (App B) — treat as an optional assumption flag, never default.

Step B — SMEARING: use the one-parameter family at u=-p^2, p in (0,M], integrated against wavefunctions f(p): if int_0^M dp f(p) C_2^imp[m^2,J](-p^2) >= 0 for all m>=M and all even J (eq 46), then int_0^M dp f(p)[8piG/p^2 + 2g_2 + g_3 p^2] >= 0 (eq 47). EXACT HEAVY ACTION of a smeared functional on a state (m^2,J): Phi_J(m^2) = int_0^M dp f(p) C_2^imp[m^2,J](-p^2) + sum_{k=4,6,...} int_0^M dp h_k(p) X_k[m^2,J](-p^2). For pure powers f=p^n these integrals are closed-form 2F1's — e.g. eq (83), verified to 30 digits: int_0^1 dp p^n C_2^imp[m^2,2] = -4(D-1) 2F1(1,(n+1)/2;(n+3)/2;-1/m^2) / ((D-2) m^4 (n+1)) + 2(3D-4)/((D-2) m^4 (n+1)) - 3(3D-2)/((D-2) m^6 (n+3)); for integer n they are ELEMENTARY: rational(1/m^2) + c * atan(1/m)/m (even n) or log-type (odd n) — verified example D=7, J=4, n=2: I(m^2) = -64/(5m^2) + 74/(15 m^4) - 177/(25 m^6) + 512/(49 m^8) - 256/(63 m^10) + 64 atan(1/sqrt(m^2))/(5 sqrt(m^2)); only (m^2+p^2)-denominators survive partial fractions (regularity at u=-m^2 kills all (m^2-p^2) terms).

IMPACT-PARAMETER INTERPRETATION (Bessel limit): in the scaling limit m->inf with b := 2J/m fixed, P_{mb/2}(1 - 2p^2/m^2) -> Gamma((D-2)/2) J_{(D-4)/2}(bp) / (bp/2)^{(D-4)/2} (eq 48; numerically verified, convergence rate O(1/m)), hence Phi_J(m^2) ~ (2 Gamma((D-2)/2)/m^4) * int_0^M dp f(p) J_{(D-4)/2}(bp)/(bp/2)^{(D-4)/2} = (2 Gamma((D-2)/2)/m^4) * fhat(b) (eq 49), where fhat is the (D-2)-dim radial Fourier transform of f(p)/p^{D-3} (eq 37). The X_k terms are subleading in this limit. So admissible functionals = compact momentum support + POSITIVE IMPACT-PARAMETER PROFILE fhat(b) >= 0 for all b — the m->inf closure of the constraint set. The graviton column in b-space is int db fhat(b) 8piG b/(D-4) (eq 38): finite iff fhat is localized, divergent in D=4 (Sec 3.7: only log-regulated bounds like eq (70), g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2) * 25 log(0.3 M b_max)). Phase 2 therefore REQUIRES D>=5 and the general-D Gegenbauer layer (verified product formula: P_J(1+y) = sum_r (y/2)^r/(r! ((D-2)/2)_r) * prod_{i=0}^{r-1}(Jc^2 - i(i+D-3)); reduces at D=4 exactly to positivity.py's _pj_taylor).
smearing:
FUNCTIONAL DESIGN SPACE (CMRS Sec 3.2 + App A.1, Table 1): f(p) = sum_n a_n p^n and h_k(p) = sum_{i=0}^{i_k} b_{k,i} p^i on support [0, M=1]; decision variables (a_n, b_{k,i}). Constraints on powers: n>1 for convergence of int f/p^2 (the G column); the LARGE-b tail of the transformed basis (eq 87: 1F2((n+1)/2; (D-2)/2, (n+3)/2; -b^2/4)/(n+1) ~ 2^n Gamma((D-2)/2)Gamma((n+1)/2)/Gamma((D-n-3)/2) * b^{-(n+1)} + oscillatory cos(b - pi(D-1)/4) * b^{-(D-1)/2}) dictates D-dependent bases so that positive-at-large-b combinations exist: D=5: {p^3-p^2, p^4-p^2, ...} (differences cancel the leading oscillation); even D>=6: {p^{3/2}, p^{5/2}, p^{7/2}, ...}; odd D>=7: {p^2, p^3, p^4, ...}. h_k in integer powers p^0, p^1, .... Fig-4 space (17-dim in even D): {p^n C_2^imp | n <= 7.5} + {p^i X_4 | i=0..5} + {p^i X_6 | i=0..3}.

HEAVY-SIDE ACTION on a state (m^2, J): Phi_J(m^2) = sum_n a_n I_n(m^2,J) + sum_{k,i} b_{k,i} I^X_{k,i}(m^2,J), with I_n = int_0^1 p^n C_2^imp[m^2,J](-p^2) dp (closed 2F1 forms, eq 83-type; elementary for integer n) and I^X from the null kernels X_k[m^2,J](u) of eq (51): X_k = (2m^2+u)/(u m^2(m^2+u)) * m^2 P_J(1+2u/m^2)/(u m^2(m^2+u))^{k/2} - Res_{u'=0}[ (2m^2+u')(m^2-u')(m^2+2u') / (m^2 (u-u') u' (m^2-u)(m^2+u')(m^2+u+u')) * m^2 P_J(1+2u'/m^2)/(u' m^2(m^2+u'))^{k/2} ]. Verified identities to build unit tests from: eq (45) C_2^imp[m^2,J](u) = C_2,0 + u C'_2,0 + u^2 X_2,u (checked J=0,2, symbolic D); X_k,u[m^2, J=0] == 0 identically for k=2,4,6 (single heavy scalar is automatically annihilated — and the end-to-end single-scalar model M = g^2 sum 1/(m0^2 - x) satisfies eq (44) exactly: low side 2g_2 - g_3 u with g_2=g^2/m0^6, g_3=3 g^2/m0^8 equals c * kernel[m0^2,0], c = g^2/m0^2); X_{2,u->0} proportional to the eq-(27) row (our n4).

POSITIVITY DOMAIN of the constraint set: Phi_J(m^2) >= 0 for ALL m^2 >= M^2 and ALL even J (eq 46/52) — a two-parameter continuum. Its closure at m->inf, b=2J/m fixed is the impact-parameter condition Gamma((D-2)/2) int_0^1 f(p) J_{(D-4)/2}(bp)/(bp/2)^{(D-4)/2} dp = fhat(b) >= 0 for all b>=0 (eq 85; h_k subleading, absent). IMPORTANT SIGN FACTS (verified numerically): f(p) itself need NOT be positive (D=5 basis elements p^3-p^2 < 0 on (0,1) are admissible), and p-space positivity is NOT sufficient: f(p)=p^4 >= 0 in D=7 has fhat(b) < 0 near b~6-9 (min ~ -0.008 at b=7), hence violates the m->inf constraints. The paper's positivity archetype eq (54): int_0^1 (1-p)cos(pb) dp = (1-cos b)/b^2 >= 0 (sympy-exact). The extremal fhat(b) concentrates at b ~ 1/M (Fig 5) — this is the mechanism that suppresses the graviton column int db fhat 8piG b/(D-4) down to O(8piG/M^2), which is where the finite gravitational bounds come from.
sdp_mapping:
HOW CMRS MAKE IT FINITE (App A) + SOUNDNESS LEDGER per step (our one-sided-direction analysis):
(1) Truncate f, h_k to finitely many basis powers — RESTRICTS the functional search space: SOUND (can only weaken the bound). 
(2) Spin truncation J in {0,2,...,J_max} (42 for Fig 4, 150 for Fig 6) — DELETES constraints on the functional: UNSOUND (same failure mode as our J_max=2 gt_3 counterexample; the returned f can violate eq (46) at J>J_max and the 'bound' is then not a theorem). Needs audit.
(3) m-discretization: parametrize m^2 = 1/(1-x), x in [0,1), initial grid x in {0, delta_x, 2delta_x, ...} (eq 84; delta_x = 1/400), then ADAPTIVE REFINEMENT (Sec A.2): solver output generically dips negative between grid points (saturated constraints), so they locate dips, add N=10 finer points around each, re-run SDPB until dips shrink to ~1e-10 ('outer approximation'). DELETES constraints: UNSOUND; refinement is a heuristic mitigation, NOT a certificate (their own Fig 8 shows a residual negative region (10.7298112,10.7298134), min -1.83e-10). Needs audit.
(4) Impact-parameter constraints: for b >= B (=40): write the transformed action as A(b) + B(b)cos(b - pi(D-1)/4) + C(b)sin(...) (eq 88) and REPLACE by the 2x2 PSD condition [[A+B, C],[C, A-B]] >= 0 (eqs 89-90) — STRONGER than (88): sound as an added restriction; entries expanded in 1/b to m_max (=2) subleading orders and turned into a polynomial matrix (after b-substitution when n != (D-3)/2 mod 1) — a genuine SDPB PositiveMatrixWithPrefactor block, i.e. our existing PMP machinery with 2x2 blocks. CAVEAT: the 1/b TRUNCATION of A,B,C is an approximation in neither guaranteed direction — solver-side helper only. For b in [eps_b, B]: discrete grid (eq 91, eps_b=1/250, delta_b=1/32) + refinement — same unsoundness class as (3), and anyway eq (85) is only the closure of the true constraints: imposing it is sound, relying on it is not needed.
(5) The p-integrals are CLOSED FORM (eq 83 2F1s / elementary): no quadrature error at all — assert generator-vs-mpmath agreement at import (our eq-83 check passed at 30 digits).
(6) LP solved with SDPB (precision 768, Fig 4; dualityGap/primal/dualError 1e-80 for Fig 6) — the per-(m,J) constraints are LINEAR in (a_n, b_{k,i}): 1x1 blocks at sampled points + the 2x2 asymptotic block; identical plumbing to our pmp2sdp+SDPB pipeline.

OUR A-POSTERIORI EXACT AUDIT (the object that makes the bound sound — extends positivity.py's Sturm audit): round (a_n, b_{k,i}) to exact rationals (mantissa-exact as in _mpf_to_rational). The audited object is, PER SPIN J, the exact heavy action E_J on the FULL continuum m >= M — not the grid. Structure theorem (verified): because C_2^imp[m^2,J](u) is regular at u=-m^2 (even J, any D), partial fractions in q=p^2 leave only polynomial terms and (m^2+q)-denominators, so for integer powers E_J(m) = R_0(w) + R_1(w) atan(w) + R_2(w) log(1+w^2) with w := 1/m in (0,1] and R_i in Q(w) with rational coefficients (example above; even n -> atan, odd n -> log). Exact positivity procedure: sandwich atan(w) and log(1+w^2) between alternating-series rational polynomial bounds on [0,1] (error terms sign-definite, order chosen to beat the functional's margin), reduce E_J >= 0 to POLYNOMIAL positivity of the lower sandwich on w in (0,1] — decided EXACTLY by the existing Sturm/sqf machinery (count_roots on [0,1]); factor out the w^4 leading decay before testing the w->0 endpoint. Domain: w in (0,1] == m in [M, inf), ALL of it — this replaces CMRS's x-grid+refinement with a proof. For half-integer n (even D) substitute p = q^2 and w-surds: same elementary class, or fall back to certified interval arithmetic (arb) with directed rounding — still an audit, slightly weaker aesthetics. J-coverage: per-spin exact for J <= j_audit (e.g. 200); J-tail: Phase-2a ships SCOPED ('spectra with all spins J <= j_audit', explicit, never silent) exactly like the current deg_X>2 cases; Phase-2b upgrades to a complete tail proof using the verified large-J structure: the P'_J(1)-term coefficient in C_2^imp at u=-p^2 is +4p^6/(m^6(m^4-p^4)) (positive), giving E_J = Jc^2 * T(m) + bounded(m) with T(m) elementary and |bounded| controlled via |P_J(x)|<=1 on [-1,1] (argument 1-2p^2/m^2 is in [-1,1] since p<=M<=m) — then a Cauchy-style spin cutoff per m, exactly the pattern of the existing strip tail proof. The 2x2 b-space block and all grids never enter the certificate; they only shape the candidate. 3-way discipline preserved: optimal-with-audit / scoped / hard error.
staging:
PHASE-2a — 'one genuinely gravitational number', minimal build. Choice of D: D=7 (odd D => integer powers p^2..p^7, Table 1), so all smearing integrals are elementary rational+atan/log and the audit is fully exact-arithmetic — no half-integer-power machinery. TARGET (from Table 3 / Fig 4, units 8piG=1, M=1): reproduce and certify the D=7 inequalities  g_2 - g_3/3 + 18.0717 >= 0  (max-g_3 ray)  and  g_2 + 0.129850 g_3 + 2.75853 >= 0  (near-vertical ray); restored units: g_2 + alpha g_3 M^2 + c * 8piG/M^2 >= 0. Program: fix alpha_3/alpha_2 (the g_3/g_2 column ratio) to the published ray, normalize alpha_2 = 1, minimize alpha_G; the certified statement is 'for any UV-completable scalar+gravity EFT in D=7: g_2 + alpha g_3 M^2 >= -c_cert * 8piG/M^2' with c_cert from the exact audit. Convergence yardstick: CMRS's 17(=16 in odd D)-dim space, J_max=42, delta_x=1/400, B=40, m_max=2, precision 768 gave the table values to ~1e-4 convergence and 'within a few percent of optimal'. Validation rungs BEFORE gravity (all reusable): (i) reproduce the analytic no-gravity D-general bound eq (34)-(35): -kappa(D)/M^2 < g_3/g_2 <= 3/M^2, kappa(D) closed form — sharp anchor for the general-D row layer; (ii) reproduce Fig-3-type g_3,g_4 bounds in D=6 with smeared functionals matching the forward-limit method (CMRS's own consistency check); (iii) single-scalar and eq-(56)/(83) kernel assertions at import (already-written checks become the unit tests).
CHANGES (new module qgse/verifiers/gravity_positivity.py, positivity.py untouched; shared helpers factored): [1] general-D layer: _pj_taylor_D (verified product formula), Jc^2 = J(J+D-3), Gegenbauer explicit polys; n_J^(D) is NOT needed for constraints (positive prefactor per (m,J) — drops out of per-state positivity). [2] kernel builders C2imp/Xk (eqs 44, 51) + import-time assertion battery: eq (39)-(41) residue table incl. the (-1,-1,+1) graviton pieces and lambda3^2/lambda4 zeros; eq (42)->(43) cancellation; eq (44) geometric-resummation identity; eq (45); eq (56); X_k[J=0]==0; X_2,0 = (2/(D(D-2))) * eq-(27) row; regularity at u=-m^2; D=4 reduction == legacy rows. [3] exact smearing-integral generator (sympy apart in q=p^2 + integrate; assert against eq (83) numerics at 30 digits). [4] LP assembly: NEW COLUMNS (alpha_G, alpha_2, alpha_3) as exact rationals sum a_n/(n-1), 2 sum a_n/(n+1), sum a_n/(n+3); constraint rows = Phi_J at x-grid x=1-1/m^2 (init delta_x=1/400) for J=0..42 + b-grid + the 2x2 large-b PMP block (pmp2sdp JSON already supports matrix blocks); adaptive-refinement loop around the existing _solve. [5] audit: _audit_gravity — rationalize, per-spin E_J(w) sandwich (atan/log alternating-series rational bounds) + Sturm on w in (0,1], w^4 factor-out at w->0, margin backoff in the SOUND direction (increase the reported constant c, i.e. weaken), scoped J <= j_audit result dict with explicit scope string; hard error otherwise. Effort: assertions+kernels 1-2d, integral generator 1-2d, LP+refinement 2-3d, 2x2 b-block 1-2d, audit 3-4d, validation vs kappa(D)+Table 3 2d => ~10-14 focused days.
PHASE-2b — full smearing program: even-D half-integer bases (p=q^2 audit substitution or certified interval arithmetic), D=5 difference basis, D=5..12 Table-3 sweep and Fig-4 region reconstruction by ray scan (theta in {0,...,39pi/20} around an interior point); the 93-dim Fig-6 space ({p^n C2imp, n=1.5..27.5} + X_4..X_14, J_max=150, delta_x=1/800, extra near-threshold x-constraints at x in {1-4/J,...} for J=36..150, precision 840); COMPLETE J-tail proof (Jc^2-leading-term positivity T(m) + uniform |P_J|<=1 remainder bounds + per-m Cauchy spin cutoff) upgrading 'scoped' to 'all spins'; string-amplitude regression targets INSIDE the region (eq 62-64: e.g. g_2 m^2/8piG = 3/16, g_3 m^4/8piG = 3/4 for the superstring point; -0.75/-2.07 bosonic); optional extensions behind explicit assumption flags: C_4^improved (eq 57) for g_4-with-gravity, C_0^improved SUSY tower (eqs 71-79, target 0 <= g_0 <= 3.000 * 8piG/M^6 in D=10 vs 2 zeta(3) ~ 2.40 for the superstring — needs the stronger Regge assumption s^2 M -> 0), Appendix-B extended u-range (meromorphy assumption). Estimate 4-6 weeks. Fitness hook for the search engine: assumption-space = choice of D, functional basis, null tower depth, optional axioms — same AdmissibilityGate pattern as the modular campaign.

traps.name: graviton pole-subtraction bookkeeping (the -1,-1,+1 split)
traps.description: The three crossing images st/u, su/t, tu/s contribute -G8/u, -G8/u, +G8/u respectively to the k=2 arc (sympy-verified): each piece carries the FULL pole magnitude and one enters with opposite sign. Any 'simplification' — treating only st/u as 'the graviton pole', subtracting the s-channel graviton from M_low before taking arcs, or symmetrizing with a factor guess — silently changes the pole coefficient (factor 3, factor 2, or sign) and every gravitational bound scales linearly with it. Equally silent: assuming G leaks into k>=4 cells (it does not).
traps.verification: sympy: with t=-s-u and R(expr,k) := residue_{s=0}[(2s+u)/(s(s+u)) * expr/(s(s+u))^{k/2}], assert R(G8*s*t/u,2) == -G8/u; R(G8*s*u/t,2) == -G8/u; R(G8*t*u/s,2) == +G8/u; R(G8*(st/u+su/t+tu/s),2) == -G8/u exactly (no analytic remainder: series coeffs u^r, r>=0 all zero); and R(G-term,k) == 0 for k=4,6. (check1_lowside.py — passed.)

traps.name: u-channel sign slip under u = -s-t
traps.description: One sign error substituting u=-s-t (e.g. writing su/t -> +s(s+t)/t, or the CHVD-style -g^2[1/s+1/t-1/(s+t)] pattern misapplied to the cubic graviton numerators) flips the graviton pole to +8piG/u; the SDP then happily returns a 'bound' with the gravity column inverted — bounds flip from lower to upper with no solver-side symptom.
traps.verification: two exact assertions at import: (a) crossing: build M3(S,T,U) symbolically and assert M3 invariant under all 6 permutations, THEN substitute U=-S-T and assert M_low(s,u) == M_low(u,s) after t-elimination; (b) regression: assert the full C_2,u residue equals -8piG/u + 2g2 - g3*u + 8g4*u^2 - 2g5*u^3 + 24g6*u^4 - 4g7*u^5 term-by-term (eq 39), in particular coeff(u,-1) == -8piG. (check1_lowside.py — passed.)

traps.name: spin-0 / spin-2 massless-pole mixing (lambda3^2 treated like G)
traps.description: The massless SCALAR exchange -lambda3^2[1/s+1/t+1/u] looks structurally identical to the graviton term but contributes EXACTLY ZERO to every k>=2 arc (its k=2 residue cancels between the 1/s double-pole derivative and the 1/t,1/u pieces). Wrongly assigning it a column (or 'improving' it away like g_4) contaminates the G column; conversely assuming the G-pole also cancels loses gravity entirely. Related: adding the graviton as a fake heavy state at m^2->0 in the average double-counts the pole.
traps.verification: sympy: assert R(-l3sq*(1/s+1/t+1/u), k) == 0 and R(-l4, k) == 0 identically in u for k=2,4,6 (residue AND all u-series coefficients); assert the X_k heavy kernels are G-independent by checking R(G-term, k) == 0 for all k>=4 to the working u-order, so <X_k>=0 stays exactly null in the gravitational theory. (check1_lowside.py — passed: l3sq row all zeros.)

traps.name: smearing-measure positivity confusion (p-space vs b-space)
traps.description: Neither direction of the naive positivity intuition holds: admissible f(p) need not be >= 0 (the D=5 basis p^3-p^2 is negative on (0,1)), and f(p) >= 0 does NOT make the functional admissible — positivity must hold state-by-state (eq 46) including the m->inf Bessel closure fhat(b) >= 0. A pipeline that only checks f >= 0, or only checks the finite grid, certifies false bounds; a pipeline that requires f >= 0 cannot even represent the D=5 extremal functionals.
traps.verification: two computable tests (both run, both behave as claimed): (a) sympy-exact: int_0^1 (1-p)cos(pb) dp == (1-cos b)/b^2 >= 0 (eq 54 archetype: sign-changing integrand, positive transform); (b) mpmath counterexample: in D=7, f(p)=p^4 >= 0 has fhat(b) = 1F2(5/2; 5/2, 7/2; -b^2/4)/5 < 0 at b=6..9 (min ~ -0.0082 at b=7) — assert the audit REJECTS the functional f=p^4 despite manifest p-space positivity by evaluating the exact kernel action at (J, m=2J/7) for large J and finding negativity.

traps.name: D-dependence smuggled in from the D=4 code (Legendre / X=J(J+1) / D=4 IR)
traps.description: Three sub-traps: using D=4 Legendre P_J or X=J(J+1) rows in D>4 (wrong g_3 row: correct is (3 - 4Jc^2/(D-2))/m^6, Jc^2=J(J+D-3), eq 25 — reduces to (3-2X) only at D=4); using the D=4 null rows instead of the D-general eq-(27) family; and running gravity at D=4 at all — the b-space graviton measure 8piG*b/(D-4) (eq 38) diverges, only b_max-regulated statements exist (eq 70), so a D=4 gravitational 'bound' without a regulator flag is meaningless.
traps.verification: import-time asserts (all verified): (a) u-derivative of the eq-(23) k=2 heavy kernel at u=0 equals -(3 - 4Jc^2/(D-2))/m^6 symbolically in D (matches eq 25 with sign from -g3*u); (b) X_{2,u->0}[m^2,J] * m^8 * D(D-2)/2 == Jc^2(2Jc^2 - 5D + 4) for J=2,4 symbolic D (eq 27 anchor), and its D=4 value == n4-row/4; (c) general-D P_J(1+y) product formula reduces at D=4 to the legacy _pj_taylor exactly for J=0,2,4; (d) hard error (never a number) if a gravity spec requests D=4 without an explicit b_max assumption flag.

traps.name: order of limits: t->0 Taylor / J_max truncation vs the (m,J) continuum
traps.description: Two faces of one mistake. (i) Taylor-functional face: the k=2 family cannot be Taylorized at t=0 with G != 0 (the low side is Laurent, the (2,-1) cell is off-lattice); code that silently series-expands B_2(t) drops the pole and produces finite garbage. (ii) Constraint-deletion face: sampling (m,J) on a grid with J <= J_max deletes the near-continuum Bessel regime b=2J/m ~ const where positivity is an independent constraint; SDPB then returns functionals that dip negative between samples (CMRS's own Fig 8: dips ~ -0.008 pre-refinement, residual -1.8e-10 after) — a spurious certificate if trusted without an exact audit. Refinement reduces but never proves; the roles are reversed relative to primal bootstrap intuition, so 'more constraints = safer' applies to the AUDIT, not the solve.
traps.verification: (i) assert the row generator REFUSES Taylor extraction whenever the k=2 g-side has a nonzero u^{-1} coefficient: feed M_low with G8 != 0 and expect a raised error; feed G8=0 and expect the legacy rows (regression: coeff(u,-1) == -8piG when G8 != 0). (ii) regression harness: solve the D=7 LP with J_max=2 and no b-block, then run the exact per-spin audit at J=4..40 and assert it FAILS (raises 'spurious bound refused'), mirroring the existing gt_3 J-truncation counterexample; additionally verify the Bessel closure is a genuine limit: |P_J(1-2p^2/m^2) - Gamma((D-2)/2)J_nu(bp)/(bp/2)^nu| -> 0 like O(1/m) at fixed b=2J/m (numerically verified: errors 0.019/0.0075/0.0037 at m=40/100/200), so no finite J_max ever covers it.
